content.text: How can I traverse my custom tree structure and set the name attribute to None for every internal node?

To accomplish this, we will utilize a visitor pattern that systematically traverses the entire tree. We define the action (setting the name) and restrict its application using a predicate to only process nodes that are not leaves.

```python
def remove_internal_names(self):
        """
        Set the name of all non-leaf nodes in the subtree to None.
        """
        self.visit(lambda n: setattr(n, 'name', None), lambda n: not n.is_leaf)
```

This approach efficiently targets all non-leaf structures by checking if `n` is not a leaf. Remember that this transformation modifies the structure in place, affecting how those internal nodes are referenced.
What happens if an internal node already has its name attribute set to None?
The function will simply execute setattr(n, 'name', None) again. Since it is a deterministic operation, running it multiple times on the same node has no adverse effect.
Got it! The visitor pattern makes this much cleaner.
